rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture and attach generated annex content only to a valid owner scope without changing structural elements.
Keep type-scope contract content with the component type and implementation-scope binding content with the component implementation.
Use the analyzed AADL model as the authority for valid owners, names, features, subcomponents, and connections during fusion.
generic_rules: Do not move, rename, or alter existing components, features, connections, or properties when inserting annex content.
Only merge content into a scope that can legally reference the symbols used there.
Preserve existing text and structure outside the inserted annex or annex update.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scope content should appear within the implementation body and only after referenced structural declarations are in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place content that references subcomponent paths into a component type scope.
Do not fuse clauses using unresolved names, AADL property names, or unit tokens as if they were in AGREE expression scope.
irrelevant_topics: requirement-specific range semantics beyond owner/scope legality
